seoTitle: San Remo vs Sanctuary Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between San Remo and Sanctuary Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: San Remo vs Sanctuary Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in San Remo and Sanctuary Point.
heading: San Remo vs Sanctuary Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between San Remo, New South Wales and Sanctuary Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: San Remo has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Sanctuary Point (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is San Remo or Sanctuary Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: San Remo has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Sanctuary Point scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: San Remo scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Sanctuary Point scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in San Remo or Sanctuary Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. San Remo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
